evals.prompt:
Find business and personal emails for these 5 people:
1) Topher Conway, Managing Partner at SV Angel — linkedin.com/in/topherc
2) Pete Koomen, General Partner at Y Combinator — linkedin.com/in/petekoomen
3) Kaushik Iska, Engineer at ClickHouse — linkedin.com/in/kaushikiska
4) Eric Migicovsky, Partner at Y Combinator — linkedin.com/in/ericmigicovsky
5) Honglei Liu, Founder at TofuHQ — linkedin.com/in/honglliu
evals.expected_output: A table or structured output listing all 5 people with their business emails (from Crustdata enrichment) and personal emails (from GitHub commit history or web search fallbacks). Technical people should have GitHub-sourced personal emails where possible. Source attribution for each email found.
expectations: Calls crustdata_people_enrich with fields including business_email
Batches all LinkedIn URLs into a single crustdata_people_enrich call (not one per person)
Searches for GitHub profiles for people identified as technical (Kaushik, Eric, Honglei)
Verifies GitHub profile identity before extracting commit emails
Does not search GitHub for non-technical people (Topher as VC)
Presents results in a clear table with source attribution for each email found

evals.prompt:
I met these people at a conference. Can you find their business emails?
1) Ali Kashani, CEO of Serve Robotics
2) Will Drevno, Head of Engineering at Lyft
3) Michael Ma, Partner at Liquid 2 Ventures
4) Priya Sharma, ML Engineer at Google DeepMind
evals.expected_output: Resolves LinkedIn profiles for all 4 people using Crustdata people search (since no LinkedIn URLs provided), then enriches with business emails. Reports which people were found and which could not be resolved.
expectations: Uses crustdata_people_search_db to resolve LinkedIn profiles from names and companies
Falls back to crustdata_web_search for people not found in the people database
Includes company context in searches to disambiguate common names
Proceeds to business email enrichment after resolving LinkedIn URLs
Reports which people could not be resolved and why

evals.prompt:
I need personal emails (gmail, etc.) for these engineers I want to recruit. Business emails won't work since I'm reaching out personally:
1) Kaushik Iska, PeerDB/ClickHouse engineer
2) Pete Koomen, previously at Optimizely
3) Honglei Liu, Head of ML at Twitter / TofuHQ founder
evals.expected_output: Finds personal emails for all 3 technical people via GitHub commit history extraction. For each person: finds GitHub profile, verifies identity, locates oldest non-fork repo, extracts commit author email. Falls back to web search (Codeforces, personal sites) when GitHub doesn't yield results.
expectations: Searches for GitHub profiles for all 3 people (all are technical)
Finds oldest non-fork repositories for GitHub commit email extraction
Validates extracted emails are not noreply/generated addresses
Falls back to web search (Codeforces, personal sites, etc.) when GitHub doesn't yield a personal email
Clearly indicates which emails are personal vs business in the output
